author: Gustavo de Aguiar Martins

interpretation.boundary: This guard checks calibration semantics; policy, schema, replay and graph validators keep their own authority and topology responsibilities.
interpretation.purpose: Validate alignment between PNVA tension values, threshold margins, decision kind, guard events and action labels.
interpretation.sovereignty: A no-tick runtime is stronger when every collapse, block or observe decision can be traced to tension crossing or not crossing threshold.

recommendations: Treat native collapse below threshold as release-blocking.
Treat native block above threshold as release-blocking.
Keep canonical bridge calibration warnings visible until legacy logs are replaced by native pnva.event.v1 emission.

summary.legacy_warning_policy: canonical bridge calibration warnings are preserved as migration evidence; native runtime calibration must remain error-free
